narrative: OMX30 enters Friday session in a technical bull market (+8.4% above 200-MA) but faces significant headwinds from overnight US equity collapse and negative global correlation (-0.479%). The Stochastic K=90.6 and D=83.4 signal overbought conditions, while ADX at 9.5 (below 18 threshold) indicates weak trend conviction requiring 30% confidence reduction. USD/SEK strength (+1.98%) should benefit industrial exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson), but NASDAQ's -1.34% drop limits sentiment for tech/telecom names. Banks gain from elevated US yields (4.487% +54bps), but commodities weakness hits Boliden and SSAB. Risk-off momentum is minimal given VIX compression (-7.48%), limiting defensive rotation into pharma. Current technicals suggest consolidation near 3,120 with mean reversion risk if support breaks.
morningBrief: OMX30 faces a mixed open: strong technical structure (above 200-MA, MACD positive, bull regime intact) clashes with overnight US weakness and negative global correlation. Stochastic overbought (K=90.6, D=83.4) flags mean reversion risk; ADX weakness (9.5) cuts conviction by 30%. USD/SEK at 9.543 (+1.98%) is a key positive for large-cap exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson B) despite NASDAQ selloff. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) should outperform on US 10Y at 4.487% supporting NIM expansion, but watch for profit-taking if yields retrace. Materials/mining (Boliden, SSAB) under pressure from commodity collapse (oil -1.95%, copper -1.37%). Key support 3,085 (lower Bollinger), resistance 3,150 (50-MA). Watch US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET for fresh direction. Bias: tight range trade 3,100–3,140, exporters favored on FX strength if risk sentiment stabilizes.
drivers: US equity weakness (-1.2% S&P, -1.3% NASDAQ) offsetting strong OMX30 technicals
USD/SEK strength (+1.98%) supporting exporters but limited by negative global correlation (-0.479%)
Stochastic overbought (K=90.6) and weak ADX (9.5) signal mean reversion risk despite bull regime
US 10Y yield +54bps supporting banks but commodities weakness (-1.95% Brent, -1.61% gold) pressuring materials

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +54bps to 4.487% widens NIM expectations; Stochastic overbought may limit upside.
USD/SEK +1.98% to 9.543 supports export competitiveness; offset by negative global correlation and NASDAQ weakness.
Brent crude -1.95%, copper -1.37%, gold -1.61% hit input costs and direct revenue; commodity weakness trumps general USD strength.
NASDAQ -1.34% overnight pressures sentiment; Stochastic overbought may trigger tech profit-taking.
US yields up support rate-sensitive valuations but SEK appreciation neutral on refinancing. Mixed signals.
VIX compression (-7.48%) limits defensive rotation; no tail-risk premium justifying rotation away from cyclicals.

watchList.reason: USD/SEK +1.98% is a structural positive for truck/bus exporter; Stochastic overbought on OMX30 may create dips.
US 10Y +54bps expands NIM; Swedish bank underperformance vs European peers (DAX +0.04%, CAC +0.04%) suggests relative value.
Gold -1.61%, copper -1.37%, despite USD strength commodity rout dominates; risk-reward poor into weakness.
NASDAQ -1.34% overnight, Stochastic K=90.6 overbought on OMX; mean reversion risk into resistance.
EUR/SEK +0.84% raises sourcing costs but oil -1.95% lowers input pressure; offsetting forces.

econCalendarAlert: US Initial Jobless Claims (week ended 13 June) at 14:30 CET — key inflation/Fed policy signal. Swedish data light. Watch for ECB communications.
topRisk: Stochastic K=90.6 and D=83.4 overbought condition combined with weak ADX (9.5) = mean reversion trap. If OMX30 closes below 3,100, Bollinger mean reversion could accelerate to 3,085. Overnight NASDAQ weakness (-1.34%) may trigger panic liquidation in tech/telecom. US Jobless Claims surprise could spark fresh risk-off if layoffs accelerate. Iran-US deal initially positive but geopolitical tail risks remain.
technicalSummary: MACD above signal line (hist=+4.582) — bullish momentum structure
Stochastic OVERBOUGHT: K=90.6, D=83.4 — mean reversion risk, reduce confidence by 30% per ADX rule
ADX=9.5 (weak, below 18 threshold) — trend conviction weak; bullish DI bias noted but unreliable
Bollinger %B=0.80 (upper half) — price stretched; width 4.66% suggests volatility contraction
RSI-14=55.36 (neutral zone) — no extreme overbought/oversold pressure
ROC-90=+1.6% (neutral) — medium-term momentum stable but not accelerating
52W positioning: 94% of range, -1.6% from high — near cycle top, distribution risk
